results.possibleIdentity: Edward McCarthy was a technical professional active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, serving as a programmer and systems analyst. He was employed by Computer Applications Inc. (CAI), a prominent independent software and consulting firm based in New York City (headquartered at 555 Madison Avenue). At CAI, McCarthy held a leadership role, identified in trade publications as the Chief of the Computer Applications Division. He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Whitman to bridge the gap between the arts and engineering. McCarthy's involvement likely stemmed from E.A.T.'s Technical Services Program, which maintained a directory and index cards to match engineers and scientists with artists for collaborative projects. His professional background in systems analysis and programming at one of the era's largest software firms made him a typical candidate for such interdisciplinary collaborations during the peak of the E.A.T. movement.
